channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics for tumor microenvironment modulation in solid tumor therapy
item.description: Multifunctional nanotherapeutics represent an emerging approach for modulating the tumor microenvironment in solid tumor treatment. These nanotechnology-based therapeutics are designed to target and alter the cellular and molecular conditions surrounding tumors, potentially improving therapeutic eff

item.title: Image-guided activation of drugs with electromagnetic radiation
item.description: Image-guided pharmacotherapy holds great promise for personalized medicine through confining drug activity precisely to the disease area using electromagnetic irradiation. This Perspective describes the molecular principles behind drug activation using electr…

item.title: Zero-shot design of drug-binding proteins via neural iterative selection−expansion
item.description: By pairing two neural networks in an iterative optimization algorithm, small-molecule binding proteins can be designed from scratch with high accuracy, affinity and success rates, showing promise for applications in drug delivery and sequestra…

item.title: New york democrat micah lasher wins house primary that drew big spending from AI groups
item.description: New York Assemblyman Micah Lasher has won the Democratic nomination in the 12th Congressional District, NBC News projects, benefitting from his deep ties to the area and backing from party leaders in a primary that garnered national attention.

item.title: Canada's AI strategy aims to make health data more accessible. how will it protect privacy?
item.description: Connecting health data across the country will help researchers conduct clinical trials, test AI health tools and drive innovation in treatment and diagnosis, says federal Minister of Artificial Intelligence Evan Solomon. But the initiative will also have to …

item.title: Show hn: sql mcp server – 61.37% on dataagentbench with glm-5.2
item.description: We just posted results to the DataAgentBench leaderboard scoring 61.37% with GLM 5.2. Please check it out and do share your feedback Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48656497 Points: 1 # Comments: 0

item.title: What is the betterhelp scandal? is betterhelp legit? everything you've ever wanted to know about the online therapy giant
item.description: BetterHelp has grown into the world's largest online therapy platform by doing something that the traditional mental health care system has long struggled to accomplish: making licensed therapy accessible to people who might otherwise never pursue it.

item.title: Digital sovereignty is no longer optional - agentic AI has made it fundamental
item.description: How business can prepare for Agentic AI.

item.title: Dietary cholesterol activates a ral-dependent pathway driving ldlr turnover
item.description: Chronic dietary cholesterol activates Ral GTPases, which promote LDLR internalization and lysosomal degradation through RalBP1–REPS1 and CTSA, thereby reducing cholesterol clearance, whereas CTSA inhibition restores LDLR function and may offer a therapeutic s…

item.title: Fusobacterium periodonticum promotes colorectal tumorigenesis via decanoic acid-driven neutrophil chemotaxis
item.description: Here, using a multi-omics approach, the authors reveal that Fusobacterium periodonticum is enriched in colorectal cancer and correlates with elevated decanoic acid, driving neutrophil chemotaxis via a G-protein-dependent mechanism to promote colorectal tumori…

item.title: Disparate privacy risks from medical AI
item.description: AI models for medical diagnostics are vulnerable to membership inference attacks.

item.title: Facilitating structure-based drug discovery with an artificial intelligence-driven virtual screening platform
item.description: This protocol describes an artificial intelligence-driven virtual screening platform designed to facilitate structure-based drug discovery. The platform integrates AI methods to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of identifying promising drug candidates from large molecular databases through comput

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

item.title: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
item.description: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new…

item.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
item.description: The silicon race is heating up amid the struggle to keep up with demand.

item.title: Unveiling the molecular mechanism of Qingwen Baidu decoction against dengue fever: an integrated study of bioinformatic analysis, machine learning and network pharmacology
item.description: This study employed bioinformatic analysis, machine learning, and network pharmacology to elucidate the molecular mechanisms by which Qingwen Baidu decoction, a traditional herbal formulation, exerts activity against dengue fever. The integrated computational approach identified key molecular target

item.title: STAT+: A sweeping new AI to detect heart conditions is coming to OpenEvidence
item.description: EchoNext, a new AI tool cleared by the FDA, will let doctors use an EKG to easily screen patients for structural heart diseases.

item.title: Will AI talent departures hurt alphabet's long-term growth?
item.description: Alphabet shares recorded their steepest one-day decline in more than a year after another prominent AI researcher departed Google DeepMind. The selloff wiped out over $225 billion in market value as investors questioned the company's ability to retain top art…

item.title: Efficient generation of epitope-targeted antibodies with germinal
item.description: Germinal achieves epitope-targeted, de novo complementarity-determining region design with high experimental success rates.

item.title: UN urges AI firms to come clean on their environmental costs
item.description: The United Nations wants artificial-intelligence companies to stop treating the environmental bill as somebody else’s problem. In a call amplified this week by Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, the organisation is pressing the firms behind the AI boom to di…

item.title: Greece opens public consultation on national framework for regulating AI under new EU law
item.description: The draft legislation sets out Greece’s national system for implementing the EU Artificial Intelligence Act, including oversight bodies, complaint procedures, penalties and a regulatory sandbox for start-ups and SMEs The post Greece opens public consultation …

item.title: Bright evening light tied to macular degeneration, glaucoma, and cataracts
item.description: In a massive study of more than 80,000 adults, scientists have found a link between bright artificial light in the evenings and age-related eye disease. At the extreme, light exposure was associated with a 31% higher risk of age-related macular degeneration (…

item.title: Fiftieth anniversary conference marking the 1976 drought
item.description: Environment Agency Chief Executive Philip Duffy’s keynote address looking at how the devastating drought changed the way we manage and use water.

item.title: Five eyes spooks warn AI means infosec incidents can become ‘major operational and financial crises’
item.description: Bosses told to step up and get cybersecurity right

item.title: India and china are home to 2.9 billion people – and together they bought just 13 million pcs in q1
item.description: PLUS: Indian telco ponders broadband satellites; Samsung goes all-in on OpenAI; Vietnam centrally plans ten tech giants; and more!

item.title: Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without ray-ban
item.description: For the past three years, "Meta" and "Ray-Ban" have been synonymous in the smart glasses space. Not anymore. Yesterday, I slipped on several pairs of Meta Glasses - no Ray-Bans - in three different styles and seven colors. One style, I was told several times …

item.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI | techcrunch
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA reforming clinical trials, a Pfizer setback, and much more news
item.description: The latest pharmaceutical news from the Pharmalot campus via the Pharmalittle newsletter

item.title: STAT+: OpenEvidence will add FDA-cleared AI to detect heart disease
item.description: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: OpenEvidence adds AI to detect heart disease, and Cadence raises $100 million, and more.

item.title: HHS has sent drug for Ebola clinical trial
item.description: FDA's early-stage clinical trial program, a mysterious single patient gets retatrutide access, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

item.title: Nvidia bets on agentic AI to turbocharge biotech discovery
item.description: Artificial intelligence played a prominent role at this week’s Bio International Convention in San Diego, the largest biotech event with vendors spanning the full ecosystem of companies in this industry. Today in a special address, Kimberly Powell (pictured),…

item.title: Drug repurposing of fluvastatin as a dp2 antagonist: an ensemble learning strategy with in vitro cellular functional validation
item.description: RSC Med. Chem., 2026, Accepted ManuscriptDOI: 10.1039/D6MD00403B, Research ArticleGuoHui Wei, Zhuo Sun, Yuanxu Gao, Jia Heng, Wenyang Lu, Zixing ZouWe present a framework to repurpose FDA-approved drugs as DP2 antagonists for asthma, using a two-stage strateg…

item.title: Ascletis announces u.s. FDA ind clearance for phase i study of once-monthly subcutaneously administered glp-1r/gipr dual peptide agonist, asc35, for the treatment of obesity
item.description: Ascletis Announces U.S. FDA IND Clearance for Phase I Study of Once-Monthly Subcutaneously Administered GLP-1R/GIPR Dual Peptide Agonist, ASC35, for the Treatment of Obesitybiospace.com

item.title: Defections from google deepmind prompt questions about alphabet’s efforts to stay at the forefront of AI
item.description: With Google's AI models losing leaderboard places and its pace of model releases lagging, some are questioning if the internet giant can stay at AI's cutting edge.

item.title: Build a protein research copilot with amazon bedrock agentcore
item.description: This post shows you how to build a conversational protein research assistant that combines three capabilities: Natural language query parsing to extract structured search parameters, vector similarity search over protein embeddings using a specialized languag…

item.title: This startup says it saves medicare more than $2 million a week
item.description: Cadence reached a $1.2 billion valuation by using AI to track seniors’ chronic health conditions and improve their care.

item.title: From chronic pain to breast cancer, snakes are inspiring a slew of new drugs
item.description: Could the Burmese python hold the secret to weight-loss jabs that are free of side effects? That's the hope of research into the reptile's remarkable ability to suppress its appetite.

item.title: Children at risk of 'very real danger' from AI
item.description: The Children's Rights Alliance has called on Government to consider the "very real danger" that exists through the rapid advance of AI technologies without proper regulation or regard for children's safety or welfare.

item.title: Gartner: prioritise governance to beat AI hype
item.description: Gartner analysts call for IT leaders to prioritise foundational investments in governance, change management, and talent to realise the benefits of AI

item.title: The new york democratic primaries are also a battleground for the AI industry
item.description: The New York primary race for the 12th district has national implications.

item.title: Github joins coalition advocating for fixes to california AI transparency act to protect open source
item.description: We’re calling for targeted amendments to resolve conflicts with open source licensing and align with international transparency frameworks while preserving regulatory intent. The post GitHub joins coalition advocating for fixes to California AI Transparency A…

item.title: Ags health expands leadership team, deepening its commitment to rcm AI innovation and strategic growth
item.description: AGS Health Expands Leadership Team, Deepening Its Commitment to RCM AI Innovation and Strategic Growthminyanville.com

item.title: Canada health infoway releases results of national AI...
item.description: Canada Health Infoway Releases Results of National AI Scribe Program, Demonstrating Meaningful Benefits for Primary Care Clinicians Canada NewsWire.

item.title: 25 women pioneers who shaped ai's evolution
item.description: From the very first algorithm in the 1840s to World War II's ENIAC to current Mythos headlines, women can stake claim to some of the most notable advancements in AI's history.

item.title: The pitfalls of unsupervised AI
item.description: Unsupervised AI risks exposed by military operations reveal critical pitfalls in enterprise use. Learn governance strategies to address accountability gaps and oversight failures.

item.title: AI groups spend $20 million going head-to-head in new york race pitting bores, lasher, schlossberg
item.description: Companies are spending big to shape AI policies at the federal level. And the Manhattan House Democratic primary will be a test of where voters stand.

item.title: Igc pharma's aha agentic AI platform reduces alzheimer's data harmonization time by 90% in representative workflow
item.description: Patent-Pending Multi-Agent Architecture Targets Fragmented Biomedical Data and Expands Toward Imaging, Omics, and Broader Health-Data Applications Ahead of Planned AAIC 2026 Demonstration in London

item.title: Cargo: a cytometry analysis framework via regularized graph optimal-transport
item.description: Author summary Understanding the immune system requires analyzing millions of cells, each carrying unique biological signals. Traditional visualization tools, like scatter plots or popular algorithms such as t-SNE and UMAP, help simplify this complexity but o…

item.title: Canada’s biotech moment: where scientific depth is meeting commercial scale
item.description: Canada’s biotech sector is no longer relying solely on the romance of discovery. It is investing in plants, process development, translational platforms and globally relevant manufacturing. The post Canada’s biotech moment: Where scientific depth is meeting …
